(ERRONEOUSLY NAMED AS SIEMENS MOBILITY, A DIVISION OF SIEMENS AG) HEARING SET FOR SEP-30-2020 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. THE CITY SAYS PLAINTIFF HAD TO PLEAD A STATUTE GIVING IT A MANDATORY DUTY. PLAINTIFF PLED TWO SUCH STATUTES: CIVIL CODE 2100 AND 2101. (SEE LOPEZ V. SOUTHERN CAL. RAPID TRANSIT DIST. (1985) 40 CAL.3D 780, 785 N.2. ("A LIABILITY IS DEEMED 'PROVIDED BY STATUTE' [SUCH AS CIVIL CODE 2100] IF A STATUTE DEFINES THE TORT IN GENERAL TERMS").) AS AGREED, PLAINTIFF FILED A DISMISSAL OF HIS FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION, SO THE DEMURRER TO IT IS MOOT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER, CLERK: S. LE, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS FOR PLAINTIFFS FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS DEPOSITION: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S UNOPPOSED MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE. COSTS ARE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. IN ADDITION TO PREPARING AN ORDER GRANTING THE MOTION, DEFENDANT SHALL PREPARE A JUDGMENT. DEFENDANT SHALL SEND THESE DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF FOR APPROVAL AS TO FORM BEFORE SUBMITTING THEM TO THE COURT FOR SIGNATURE PER RULE 3.1312 OF THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: BRUCE HIGHMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-04-2020 Continued To Dec-23-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant Siemens Mobility, Inc. To Strike Portions Of Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SIEMENS MOBILITY, INC.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint is DENIED.

Plaintiff's sole cause of action against Siemens, the designer, manufacturer and seller of the light rail vehicle on which plaintiff was injured, is for strict liability for a defective product. Plaintiff alleges, among other things, that Siemens had knowledge that the doors to the vehicle contained a design defect wherein the doors would close, lock, trap and injure passengers (Compl. pars. 8, 9, 11, 13, 15); that it did not perform proper testing of the door sensors, despite express notice from the City (id. par. 14); that despite knowledge of the defect, it intentionally and willfully delivered the vehicle to the City (id. par. 19); and that it sold the vehicle to the City knowing that the City would put it into passenger service without installing additional safety devices or systems to prevent injuries. (Id. pars. 19, 40). These allegations are sufficient to support plaintiff's prayer for punitive damages, which are available in a strict products liability case where the defendant's conduct involved conscious disregard of the rights or safety of others. (Pfeifer v. John Crane, Inc. (2013) 220 Cal.App.4th 1270, 1299-1301 [upholding jury verdict awarding punitive damages against defendant, which knew that its products were likely to pose a danger to users, whom it did not warn].) Defendant's argument that plaintiffs must allege its knowledge with more specificity is premature at the pleading stage. (See Civ. Code Section 3294(a) [plaintiff's burden at trial is to establish oppression, fraud or malice by clear and convincing evidence]; Hoch v. Allied-Signal, Inc. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 48, 59 ["If the plaintiff is to prevail on a claim for punitive damages, the evidence will have to be clear and convincing to the trier of fact."].)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-25-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Alexander Wong for Plaintiff.
James Hannawalt (415-554-3913) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:
Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to plaintiff's first cause of action is overruled. The City says plaintiff had to plead a statute giving it a mandatory duty. Plaintiff pled two such statutes: Civil Code 2100 and 2101. (See Lopez v. Southern Cal. Rapid Transit Dist. (1985) 40 Cal.3d 780, 785 n.2. ("a liability is deemed 'provided by statute' [such as Civil Code 2100] if a statute defines the tort in general terms").) As agreed, plaintiff filed a dismissal of his fourth cause of action, so the demurrer to it is moot.

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
